title: Astragalus insularis-ashkii
taxonomy: Astragalus insularis-ashkii is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It is a part of the genus Astragalus, which comprises over 3,000 species of vetches and milkvetches. The family Fabaceae, also known as legumes, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Astragalus insularis-ashkii reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the family. This classification system is based on the principles of taxonomy and systematics in botany.

intro: Astragalus insularis-ashkii, also known as Ashkii Milk Vetch or Ashkii Astragalus, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are known to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, providing potential health benefits such as anti-fatigue, anti-aging, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus insularis-ashkii include astragalosides, flavonoids like isorhapontigenin, and phenolic acids like ferulic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Astragalus insularis-ashkii is often prepared as a decoction or extract, and is used to make herbal supplements, teas, and infusions for the treatment of various health conditions.
